exemplar.title: Learning how to Speak Dutch at A2 Level

detailedExemplarData.title: Learning how to Speak Dutch at A2 Level

strengths: Clear identification and integration of multiple ATL skills, especially Media Literacy and Self-Motivation
Concrete evidence of Self-Motivation in the calendar screenshot showing consistent scheduling
Strong Information Literacy and Organization skills demonstrated in structured vocabulary lists and side-by-side English-Dutch tables
weaknesses: ATL skill application lacks quantified measures of impact (for example, tracking progress metrics to show how scheduling or media resources accelerated learning)
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You effectively selected and documented a range of ATL skills, supporting your approach with screenshots and well-organized data tables. To deepen your analysis, introduce quantifiable measuresâsuch as vocabulary retention rates or time-on-task statisticsâthat demonstrate how these skills directly improved your learning outcomes and the quality of your Dutch phrases booklet.

strengths: Emotional and personal connection to the learning goal, rooted in childhood experience, which drives motivation and depth of engagement
Comprehensive success-criteria table with clear performance levels that will guide evaluation of the final booklet
Actionable timeline tools (mind map and Gantt chart) that synthesize research insights and link interview findings to project steps
weaknesses: Lack of measurable targets for the booklet (e.g., number of entries, quality benchmarks, user-feedback metrics) to make success criteria fully actionable
Incomplete specification rows and missing resource allocations, limiting the feasibility and clarity of the plan
Overly granular weekly breakdown that could be streamlined into critical milestones with clear dates and assigned resources
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates strong personal motivation and evidence-based design: you link childhood memories to the learning goal, develop a detailed success-criteria framework, and construct both a mind map and Gantt chart to organize tasks. To reach the highest level of planning, refine these tools by adding measurable targets for the booklet, completing all specification details with resources and deadlines, and consolidating your weekly tasks into key milestones to improve readability and manageability.

strengths: Authentic reflection on design challenges, showing candid acknowledgment of product limitations
Forward-looking discussion of how acquired skills will transfer to university and career contexts
Detailed self-assessment of personal growth attributes (Self-Drive, Patience, Responsibility)
Robust product evaluation table with quantitative survey results and critical analysis of testing methodsâ strengths and limitations
weaknesses: Reflection relies heavily on quantitative data; adding qualitative feedback from users would provide richer, more balanced insights
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflective section excels at honest self-evaluation, linking evidence from surveys and testing critiques to broader personal and academic growth. To enhance the depth of your reflection, incorporate qualitative user comments or anecdotes that contextualize the numbers and reveal nuanced perceptions of your bookletâs usability and impact.

annotations.comment: You clearly articulate a personal, emotionally resonant learning goal connected to childhood experiences; this strong context enhances motivation and aligns well with IB planning expectations.
Your candid discussion of design struggles shows authentic reflection on product limitations, which will guide targeted improvements in future iterations.
Your discussion of future applicability of skills to university and career demonstrates forward-looking reflection, enriching your evaluation of project impact.

Your product goal is clearly defined as a Dutch phrases booklet; to strengthen Planning, include measurable success criteria for the bookletâs quality, such as a target number of entries or user satisfaction metrics.
Your mind map effectively synthesizes research insights into product specifications; next, integrate resource plans and clear sequencing to develop a fully feasible plan for achieving the booklet.
You present justified specifications with clear success conditions; ensure all specification rows are completed to provide a comprehensive set of criteria guiding your product development.
You effectively link interview insights to your action plan, demonstrating evidence-based planning; to add rigor, outline specific resources and precise dates for each step.
Your Gantt chart provides a clear overview of long-term tasks; refine task descriptions and add resource allocations to transform this into a fully detailed, feasible plan.
Your weekly task breakdown shows impressive granularity; to streamline Planning, focus on critical milestones and group similar tasks, improving readability and manageability.
Your success-criteria table articulates clear performance levels for each specification, demonstrating robust measurement planning for evaluating product outcomes.
Your analysis of existing language products provides strong justification for design choices; directly link these insights to specific success criteria to reinforce your planning rationale.
You effectively identify and integrate multiple ATL skills in your learning process; your use of varied resources shows purposeful application of Media Literacy and Self-Motivation.
The calendar screenshot is effective evidence of Self-Motivation, demonstrating consistent scheduling to support your weekly Dutch learning routine.
Your inclusion of the DutchPod101 screenshot concretely shows Media Literacy in selecting authentic resources, strengthening the credibility of your learning strategy.
Well-articulated Organization and Information Literacy skills in structuring vocabulary lists; this systematic approach enhances the quality and usability of your final product.
The side-by-side EnglishâDutch tables exemplify how you applied Information Literacy by filtering and organizing data, clearly evidencing ATL skills in your product.
You describe Self-Motivation and concrete scheduling; consider tracking learning-progress metrics to quantify this ATL skillâs effectiveness in meeting your goal.
Your detailed reflection on Self-Drive, Patience, and Responsibility shows authentic self-awareness of how your project impacted personal growth, aligning strongly with Reflecting criteria.
Your product evaluation table provides quantifiable results for each success criterion, demonstrating clear evidence-based reflection on product effectiveness.
Your evaluation of testing methods critically addresses strengths and limitations, showing reflective depth in assessing how evidence was gathered and its reliability.
Inclusion of quantitative evidence (survey results and bar chart) strengthens your product evaluation; consider adding qualitative feedback for a more balanced reflection.
